commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A focused repair can extend interpreter risk classification and add renderer regressions without a product decision.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a security-sensitive approval summarizer, and it still under-labels documented interpreter eval commands in simple mode.; Review finding: [P2] Classify all documented interpreters as high risk — src/infra/plugin-approvals.ts:1179-1186 (sha=16ccf90c88f5bf88c19b89455cdf51e91b974fe5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `approvals.plugin.language` for original/simple/simple-technical plugin approval copy, wires it through shared forwarding plus Telegram/Matrix fallback paths, updates docs/config/tests/baselines, and carries oc-path formatting plus an `@grammyjs/types` alignment.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the PR only sends bash/fish/node/python/python3/ruby/sh/zsh through the high-risk interpreter path, while current security docs also call out perl/php/lua/osascript; shell syntax checks accept representative inline-eval commands for those missing interpreters.
commands.review_followup: A focused repair can extend interpreter risk classification and add renderer regressions without a product decision.
commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
